key_insight: IA-NNP learns an interface-specific Newton preconditioner that improves convergence for difficult cohesive-zone increments without changing the underlying fracture model or solution set.
engineering_relevance: It can make implicit fracture and delamination simulations more robust in composite structures by reducing Newton failures, wrong-branch convergence, and costly step cuts.
method: Lifts active interface variables using learned, state-dependent corrections from interface graph features.
Provides two variants: IA-NNP-Init for better starting guesses and IA-NNP-NL for nonlinear right preconditioning during iterations.
Uses bounded, confidence-gated corrections and only accepts updates through the original cohesive-zone Newton solve.
evidence_signal: Benchmarks on horizontal, circular, two-interface, and active-front problems show improved difficult-increment convergence, better branch recovery, and fewer failures than standard Newton and manual modification.
watch_item: Because the correction is gated and only applies to active interfaces, performance may depend on good feature design and careful integration with existing finite-element Newton workflows.
paper_title: Interface-Aware Neural Newton Preconditioning for Robust Cohesive Zone Model Simulations
